View this page

utils¶

Logging¶

Unified logging configuration for CLI and Voilà GUI.

setup_logging() initialises the spectraPyle logger hierarchy. get_logger() returns a named child logger. In Voilà, log output is redirected to avoid polluting widget output cells.

class spectraPyle.utils.log.WidgetHandler(output_widget)[source]¶

Bases: Handler

emit(record)[source]¶

Do whatever it takes to actually log the specified logging record.

This version is intended to be implemented by subclasses and so raises a NotImplementedError.

spectraPyle.utils.log.get_logger(name)[source]¶

Get a named logger under the spectraPyle hierarchy.

Parameters:

name (str) – Logger name, typically __name__ of the calling module.

Returns:

A logger instance configured by setup_logging().

Return type:

logging.Logger

spectraPyle.utils.log.setup_logging(level='INFO', log_file=None, gui_output=None)[source]¶

Initialize the spectraPyle logger with optional file and Voilà handlers.

Parameters:

  • level (str, optional) – Logging level name (e.g., ‘DEBUG’, ‘INFO’, ‘WARNING’). Default ‘INFO’.

  • log_file (Path or None, optional) – If provided, write logs to this file at DEBUG level. Default None.

  • gui_output (ipywidgets.Output or None, optional) – If provided, redirect logs to this widget for Voilà GUI display. Default None (logs to stdout via StreamHandler).

Return type:

None

Physics¶

Galactic dust extinction correction.

Applies the Gordon et al. (2023) extinction law via the dust_extinction package to correct observed spectra for Milky Way foreground dust.

spectraPyle.physics.extinction.AA_to_micron(lbd_A)[source]¶

Convert wavelengths from Angstroms to micrometers.

Parameters:

lbd_A (ndarray or float) – Wavelength in Angstroms.

Returns:

Wavelength in micrometers.

Return type:

ndarray or float

spectraPyle.physics.extinction.dustCorr_Gordon23(wave_A, ebv, Rv=3.1)[source]¶

Apply Gordon et al. (2023) galactic extinction correction.

Uses the G23 extinction model from the dust_extinction package to correct for Milky Way dust foreground reddening.

CITATION POLICY: This code uses the Gordon+23 extinction curve via the ‘dust_extinction’ Python package: https://dust-extinction.readthedocs.io/ If you use this functionality, please follow the citation policy for the G23 model, available here: https://dust-extinction.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dust_extinction/references.html

Parameters:

  • wave_A (ndarray) – Wavelength array in Angstroms.

  • ebv (float) – Foreground dust extinction E(B-V) in magnitudes.

  • Rv (float, optional) – Total-to-selective extinction ratio. Default 3.1.

Returns:

Extinction correction factor (flux multiplier, >= 1).

Return type:

ndarray
